experience.title: Substitute Teacher
bullets: Adhere to teacher provided lesson plans in all content areas
Record student behavior and work upon arrival of the classroom teacher through detailed notes
Maintain effective positive classroom management strategies from the start of the school day

experience.title: Literacy Practicum
bullets: Co-teach Tier 2 NYS ELA prep to multiple seventh and eighth grade sections
Observe 1:1 Leveled Literacy Intervention (LLI) Tier 3 instruction

experience.title: Literacy Practicum
bullets: Implemented diverse approaches to ensure both language and academic success
Facilitated a pullout second-grade small reading group intervention twice a week using Jan Richardson's model
Provided differentiated instruction to striving readers, for 3 third grade reading groups, through active questioning
Administered the DIBELS Next benchmarking assessment to 30 kindergarteners
Analyzed and administered Fountas & Pinnell Running Records to small reading groups
Participated in 3 RTI meetings by providing feedback and observations on the students serviced
Collaborated with the building Principal, Reading Teacher, and Binghamton University to facilitate a Toddler Night Out event at Clayton Avenue resulting in positive community and school relations

experience.title: Second Grade Student Teacher
bullets: Provided individualized reading instruction during morning centers through explicit phonics activities
Created and instructed lesson plans with Common Core aligned learning outcomes
Developed individualized lessons to assist students with IEPs
Collaborated with 1:1 aide and the Special Education teacher to pull small groups for math re-teaching

experience.title: Sixth Grade Science & Social Studies Student Teacher
bullets: Utilized technology such as Google Earth, various web quests, YouTube, and Power Points to support instruction
Encouraged journaling and classroom discussion based off the daily viewing of CNN Student News
Constructed and taught a seven-lesson unit on rocketry through the understanding of Newton's Three Laws
Challenged students to create their own country following discussions on cartography and region specific attributes
Oversaw and implemented positive classroom management skills through co-constructed procedures and re-teaching
Collaborated and instructed the class with the special education teacher to provide classroom modifications
Reinforced previously taught lessons with students before and after school through small group and 1:1 re-teaching

experience.title: Graduate Assistant Career Consultant
bullets: Co-teach a self-contained weekly seminar for the center's peer internship program
Mentor students regarding career documents and decisions, including resumes/cover letters, jobs, and internships
Facilitate walk-in consulting hours in a residential community to reach students earlier in their academic careers
Conduct and evaluates mock interviews for two hours each week to assist students in preparing for upcoming interviews by providing feedback and modeling best interview practice
Display knowledge of campus resources for student referrals

experience.title: Assistant Residential Coordinator
bullets: Part of an on-call rotation that is responsible for the well-being of 1,500 students
Promoted diversity through role modeling and emphasizing respect among residents
Assisted in the supervision of 15 RAs through weekly supervision meetings
Acted as the conflict mediator for assigned residence hall
Conducted 1:1 meetings with RAs and hall government leaders to support them in their role
Operated as an administrator for the campus conduct process by hearing student cases
Took leadership roles in implementing area housing programs assigned by the Assistant Director
Assisted in the execution of the RA Day of Service on MLK Day for 200 RAs by discovering local service sites
Aided a committee of 7 in developing diversity driven conference sessions during RA training
Hosted housing socials for transfer and international students

experience.title: Resident Mentor at Johnson Hall
bullets: Presented with the Johnson Hall 2013 Staff Member of the Year Award
Received a building wide educational programming award
Taught an experiential first year seminar (GST 100) for 16 freshmen residents as a teaching assistant
Designed and executed educational programming and community outreach programs
Documented residents' community service hours as part of the GST 100 course
Established an effective interpersonal relationship with residents through community building
Mediated a range of resident conflicts within the residence hall by showcasing active listening
Presented and conveyed information to residents via email, social media, public speaking, meetings, and individually
Active member of solicitation and fundraising team for annual event that raised $4,500 for scholarships

experience.title: Summer Resident Assistant and Desk Attendant
bullets: Created community-fostering activities for summer school and international students from China and Brazil
